title: [BOUNTY: 15 RTC] Implement /wallet/history endpoint (fixes #775, #886)
body:
## Implement the /wallet/history Endpoint

**Reward: 15 RTC**

### Background
The `/wallet/history` endpoint is documented in the API spec but returns 404. Multiple bug reports have been filed (#775, #886). Time to fix it.

### Task
Add a `/wallet/history` endpoint to `rustchain_v2_integrated_v2.2.1_rip200.py` that returns transaction history for a given wallet/miner ID.

### Expected Behavior
```
GET /wallet/history?miner_id=dual-g4-125&limit=50

Response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "miner_id": "dual-g4-125",
  "transactions": [
    {"type": "reward", "amount": 0.297, "epoch": 424, "timestamp": 1733..., "tx_hash": "..."},
    {"type": "transfer_in", "from": "founder_community", "amount": 10.0, "timestamp": ..., "tx_hash": "..."},
    ...
  ],
  "total": 42
}
```

### Data Sources
- `ledger` table: all transfers
- `epoch_rewards` table: mining rewards
- `balances` table: current balance

### Requirements
- Query both `ledger` and `epoch_rewards` tables
- Return unified, sorted-by-time response
- Support `limit` and `offset` pagination
- Handle unknown miner_id gracefully (empty list, not error)

Tags: api, bug-fix, flask, sqlite

title: [EASY BOUNTY: 2 RTC] Star & Share Campaign — Help Us Hit 200 Stars on RustChain!
body:
## ⭐ Star Drive — March 2026

**RustChain is at 149 stars — help us hit 200!**

### How to Earn 2 RTC

1. **Star these repos** (takes 30 seconds):
   - [Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) (149 ⭐)
   - [BoTTube](https://github.com/Scottcjn/bottube) (123 ⭐)
   - [Beacon Atlas](https://github.com/Scottcjn/beacon-skill) (86 ⭐)

2. **Share on social media** (X, LinkedIn, Dev.to, Reddit, HN — any platform):

3. **Comment here** with:

### Why Stars Matter
Stars increase GitHub discoverability. More stars = more contributors = stronger ecosystem. Every star helps.

**Tags:** stars, social-media, community, bounty, easy, hacktoberfest, open-source, good-first-issue

title: [BOUNTY] Write a BoTTube Integration Guide — 15 RTC
body:
## [BOUNTY] Write a BoTTube Integration Guide — 15 RTC

**Reward**: 15 RTC ($1.50 at reference rate)
**Difficulty**: Easy
**Status**: Open

### Description
Write a developer-focused guide or blog post about integrating with the BoTTube API. Published on Dev.to, Medium, Hashnode, or your own blog.

### Requirements
- Write an article (minimum 500 words) covering:
  - What BoTTube is and why it matters for AI agents
  - How to get an API key
  - Code examples for uploading videos
  - Code examples for commenting/engaging
- Must include working code snippets
- Must be published on a public platform (Dev.to, Medium, Hashnode, personal blog)
- Must link to bottube.ai

### How to Claim
1. Comment with your planned article outline
2. Write and publish the article
3. Reply with the published URL
4. Receive 15 RTC to your wallet

### Resources
- BoTTube: https://bottube.ai
- Python SDK: `pip install bottube`
- Existing articles: https://dev.to/scottcjn

title: [EASY BOUNTY: 3 RTC] Agent Resume Reel — Showcase Your Bot on BoTTube
body:
## Create Your Agent's Resume Reel

**Reward: 3 RTC per agent** (max 2 agents per person)

LinkedIn profiles are for humans. BoTTube resume reels are for agents.

### What To Do
1. Create a 6-8 second video showcasing what your agent does
2. Upload to [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai) with tag `#agentresume`
3. Post the link here as a comment

### What To Include
- Agent name and what it does (text overlay or narration)
- One example of it working (screenshot, demo clip, or animation)
- Where to find it (GitHub link, platform, etc.)

### Video Specs
- Max 8 seconds, max 720x720, max 2MB
- H.264 MP4 format
- Use `scripts/prepare_video.sh` from the BoTTube skill if needed

### Examples of Good Resume Reels
- "Hi, I'm WeatherBot. I check 50 cities every hour and post forecasts to BoTTube. Find me at github.com/..."
- "CodeReviewBot: I review PRs in 30 seconds. 500+ reviews, 3 languages. Hire me."
- Quick screen recording of your agent doing its thing with text overlay

### Rules
- Must be a real agent (not a hypothetical)
- Video must be on BoTTube (not YouTube/TikTok)
- One submission per agent
- Quality matters — lazy slideshows of text won't qualify

This builds the first-ever agent talent marketplace on video. Your reel lives forever on BoTTube.

Labels: `bounty`, `easy`, `bottube`, `community`

title: [BOUNTY: 8 RTC] Screen reader friendly video pages (a11y)
body:
## Screen Reader Friendly Video Descriptions + Alt Text

**Reward: 8 RTC**

### Task
Make BoTTube watch pages fully accessible to screen readers.

### Requirements
1. Add `alt` text to all video thumbnails (use video title)
2. Add `aria-describedby` linking video player to description text
3. Add structured data for video duration, upload date in screen-reader-friendly format
4. Ensure comment section is navigable with screen reader (proper heading hierarchy)
5. Add `role="region"` and `aria-label` to video player, comments, and recommendations sections

### Files to Modify
- `bottube_templates/watch.html` (2,761 lines -- the big one)
- `bottube_templates/index.html` (thumbnail grid)

### Proof
- PR with changes
- Screen reader test output (NVDA, VoiceOver, or ORCA)

Tags: accessibility, a11y, screen-reader, aria

title: [BOUNTY: 15-25 RTC] BoTTube Embed Player — Embeddable Video Widget
body:
## BoTTube Embed Player

YouTube's #1 growth hack was the embeddable player. Every blog post, tweet, or forum that embeds a video is free distribution. BoTTube needs this.

### What to Build

A minimal, embeddable video player accessible at:
```
https://bottube.ai/embed/{video_id}
```

That can be embedded anywhere via:
```html
<iframe src="https://bottube.ai/embed/VIDEO_ID" width="560" height="315" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
```

### Payout

| Milestone | Reward |
|-----------|--------|
| Working embed route + minimal player | **15 RTC** |
| Responsive design + autoplay/loop options | **+5 RTC** |
| Share button that copies embed code | **+5 RTC** |

### Requirements

1. **Single HTML page** served by Flask at `/embed/<video_id>`
2. **Minimal UI** — video player, title, BoTTube logo/watermark, "Watch on BoTTube" link
3. **URL parameters**: `?autoplay=1`, `?loop=1`, `?mute=1`
4. **Responsive** — scales to container width
5. **No external JS dependencies** — vanilla HTML5 `<video>` tag
6. **CORS headers** — must be embeddable cross-origin
7. **Lightweight** — under 10KB total page weight

### Technical Context

BoTTube is a Flask app on port 8097 at 50.28.86.153. Videos are served from `/uploads/`. The embed route would be a new Flask blueprint or route.

### Submit As

PR to [Scottcjn/bottube](https://github.com/Scottcjn/bottube) adding the embed route + template.

**Wallet:** Include your wallet address when claiming.

title: [BOUNTY: 5-10 RTC] BoTTube OEmbed Protocol — Rich Link Previews Everywhere
body:
## OEmbed Protocol for BoTTube

When someone pastes a BoTTube link on Discord, Slack, Twitter, or any platform supporting OEmbed, it should auto-expand with a rich preview (thumbnail, title, duration).

### Payout

| Milestone | Reward |
|-----------|--------|
| Working OEmbed JSON endpoint | **5 RTC** |
| OpenGraph + Twitter Card meta tags on video pages | **+5 RTC** |

### What OEmbed Is

[OEmbed](https://oembed.com/) is a standard for rich link previews. When a platform sees a URL, it checks for an OEmbed endpoint and gets structured data back.

### Endpoint

```
GET https://bottube.ai/oembed?url=https://bottube.ai/watch/VIDEO_ID&format=json

Response:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "type": "video",
  "provider_name": "BoTTube",
  "provider_url": "https://bottube.ai",
  "title": "Sophia's House for Uninformed Agents",
  "author_name": "sophia-elya",
  "thumbnail_url": "https://bottube.ai/thumbnails/VIDEO_ID.jpg",
  "thumbnail_width": 720,
  "thumbnail_height": 720,
  "html": "<iframe src='https://bottube.ai/embed/VIDEO_ID' width='560' height='315'></iframe>",
  "width": 560,
  "height": 315
}
```

### Also Add Meta Tags

On each `/watch/<video_id>` page:
```html
<meta property="og:type" content="video">
<meta property="og:title" content="Video Title">
<meta property="og:image" content="https://bottube.ai/thumbnails/VIDEO_ID.jpg">
<meta property="og:video" content="https://bottube.ai/uploads/VIDEO_ID.mp4">
<meta name="twitter:card" content="player">
<link rel="alternate" type="application/json+oembed" href="https://bottube.ai/oembed?url=...">
```

### Technical Requirements

1. **Flask route**: `/oembed` that accepts `url` and `format` params
2. **Looks up video** from URL, returns structured JSON
3. **Meta tags** added to the watch page template
4. **Discovery link** in `<head>` of watch pages

### Submit As

PR to [Scottcjn/bottube](https://github.com/Scottcjn/bottube).

**Wallet:** Include your wallet address when claiming.

title: [BOUNTY] Instagram Intelligence + AI Vision Analysis API — $200 paid in $SX token
body:
# Instagram Intelligence + AI Vision Analysis API — $200 Bounty

## What to Build

A premium Instagram intelligence API that goes beyond basic scraping — it uses **AI vision models** to analyze post images, detect account authenticity, classify content themes, and generate engagement predictions. This is the full influencer analysis stack: profile data + engagement metrics + AI-powered visual intelligence.

**This is our highest-value marketplace service** because it combines mobile proxy scraping with AI analysis — selling intelligence, not just data.

## Why This Is Worth $200

This bounty requires two hard skills combined:
1. **Mobile proxy scraping** — reliably extracting Instagram data through carrier IPs
2. **AI vision integration** — running image analysis on scraped content for deeper insights

The result is a service that competes with $500-999/month enterprise tools at micropayment prices.

## Why Mobile Proxies Are Required

Instagram's detection system assigns trust scores by IP type. Mobile carrier IPs from real 4G/5G towers get the highest trust because Instagram is overwhelmingly a mobile app (~95% of usage). Datacenter IPs are blocked instantly. Residential IPs get flagged under sustained scraping. Only real carrier IPs can reliably access profiles, posts, and images at the depth needed for AI analysis.

## Core Features

### 1. Profile Intelligence (Scraping)
- Full profile extraction: followers, following, posts count, bio, verification, category
- Engagement rate calculation (avg likes + comments / followers)
- Recent posts with engagement metrics
- Follower growth signals (growing/declining/stagnant)
- Posting frequency and pattern detection

### 2. AI Vision Analysis (The Differentiator)

Use an AI vision model (OpenAI GPT-4o, Claude, or open-source like LLaVA) to analyze the account's recent post images and return:

**Content Classification:**
```json
{
  "content_themes": ["lifestyle", "travel", "food", "fashion"],
  "content_style": "professional_photography",
  "brand_safety_score": 92,
  "content_consistency": "high"
}
```

**Account Type Detection:**
```json
{
  "account_type": "influencer",
  "niche": "travel_lifestyle",
  "confidence": 0.94,
  "signals": [
    "professional_quality_images",
    "consistent_aesthetic",
    "brand_collaboration_posts_detected",
    "engagement_pattern_matches_organic"
  ]
}
```

Account types to detect:
- `influencer` — consistent personal brand, high-quality content
- `business` — product-focused, promotional content
- `personal` — casual, mixed content, lower posting frequency
- `bot/fake` — stock photos, inconsistent style, generic content
- `meme_page` — repurposed content, text overlays
- `news_media` — article screenshots, news graphics

**Sentiment Analysis (from images + captions):**
```json
{
  "overall_sentiment": "positive",
  "sentiment_breakdown": {
    "positive": 72,
    "neutral": 20,
    "negative": 8
  },
  "emotional_themes": ["aspirational", "happy", "adventurous"],
  "brand_alignment": ["luxury", "wellness", "outdoor"]
}
```

**Fake Account Detection (AI-Enhanced):**
```json
{
  "authenticity_score": 87,
  "fake_signals": {
    "stock_photo_detected": false,
    "face_consistency": "same_person_across_posts",
    "engagement_vs_followers": "healthy",
    "comment_quality": "organic",
    "follower_growth_pattern": "natural"
  },
  "verdict": "likely_authentic"
}
```

### 3. Smart Filters

The API should support filtering and searching accounts by AI-derived attributes:

```
GET /api/instagram/discover?niche=travel&min_followers=10000&account_type=influencer&sentiment=positive&brand_safe=true
```

This lets brands find influencers matching specific criteria using AI classification — not just follower counts.

## Endpoints

```
GET /api/instagram/profile/:username
  → Basic profile data + engagement metrics

GET /api/instagram/posts/:username?limit=12
  → Recent posts with engagement data

GET /api/instagram/analyze/:username
  → FULL AI analysis: profile + vision analysis + sentiment + authenticity
  → This is the premium endpoint

GET /api/instagram/analyze/:username/images
  → AI vision analysis of recent post images only

GET /api/instagram/audit/:username
  → Fake follower / bot detection (AI-enhanced)

GET /api/instagram/discover?niche=X&type=Y&sentiment=Z
  → Search/filter accounts by AI-derived attributes
```

## Expected Response (Full Analysis)

```json
{
  "profile": {
    "username": "traveljane",
    "full_name": "Jane Smith",
    "bio": "Travel blogger | 50+ countries | Brand collabs: DM",
    "followers": 125000,
    "following": 890,
    "posts_count": 342,
    "is_verified": false,
    "is_business": true,
    "engagement_rate": 3.2,
    "avg_likes": 4000,
    "avg_comments": 120,
    "posting_frequency": "4.2 posts/week"
  },
  "ai_analysis": {
    "account_type": {
      "primary": "influencer",
      "niche": "travel_lifestyle",
      "confidence": 0.94,
      "sub_niches": ["luxury_travel", "food_travel", "photography"]
    },
    "content_themes": {
      "top_themes": ["travel", "food", "architecture", "nature"],
      "style": "professional_photography",
      "aesthetic_consistency": "high",
      "brand_safety_score": 95
    },
    "sentiment": {
      "overall": "positive",
      "breakdown": { "positive": 78, "neutral": 18, "negative": 4 },
      "emotional_themes": ["aspirational", "adventurous", "joyful"],
      "brand_alignment": ["luxury", "wellness", "outdoor", "hospitality"]
    },
    "authenticity": {
      "score": 92,
      "verdict": "authentic",
      "face_consistency": true,
      "engagement_pattern": "organic",
      "follower_quality": "high",
      "comment_analysis": "mostly_genuine"
    },
    "images_analyzed": 12,
    "model_used": "gpt-4o-vision"
  },
  "recommendations": {
    "good_for_brands": ["travel_agencies", "hotels", "airlines", "camera_brands"],
    "estimated_post_value": "$800-1200",
    "risk_level": "low"
  },
  "meta": {
    "proxy": { "ip": "174.xxx.xxx.xxx", "country": "US", "carrier": "T-Mobile" },
    "analysis_time_ms": 4200
  }
}
```

## Pricing

| Endpoint | Price |
|----------|-------|
| `/profile/:username` | $0.01 USDC |
| `/posts/:username` | $0.02 USDC |
| `/analyze/:username` (full AI) | $0.15 USDC |
| `/analyze/:username/images` | $0.08 USDC |
| `/audit/:username` | $0.05 USDC |
| `/discover` (search) | $0.03 USDC |

The full analysis at $0.15/profile vs HypeAuditor's $499/month makes this 100x more accessible.

## Technical Requirements

1. Route all Instagram requests through Proxies.sx mobile proxies via `getProxy()`
2. Extract profiles: followers, engagement, posts, bio, verification
3. Download and analyze recent post images (last 12 posts minimum)
4. Integrate an AI vision model (OpenAI GPT-4o recommended, or Claude, or open-source)
5. Implement account type classification with confidence scores
6. Content theme detection from images (not just hashtags)
7. Sentiment analysis combining image mood + caption text
8. Fake account detection using visual + engagement signals
9. Smart filters: search accounts by AI-derived attributes
10. Wire into `src/service.ts` with x402 payment flow
11. Handle Instagram rate limiting gracefully

## AI Model Integration

You can use any vision model. Recommended approach:
```javascript
// Download recent post images via mobile proxy
const images = await fetchRecentImages(username, proxy);

// Send to vision model for analysis
const analysis = await openai.chat.completions.create({
  model: "gpt-4o",
  messages: [{
    role: "user",
    content: [
      { type: "text", text: "Analyze these Instagram posts..." },
      ...images.map(img => ({ type: "image_url", image_url: { url: img } }))
    ]
  }]
});
```

You'll need your own API key for the vision model. Include the cost in your x402 pricing.

## Submission Requirements

- [ ] Live deployment URL
- [ ] Real AI analysis for 5+ public Instagram accounts (mix of influencers, businesses, personal)
- [ ] Account type detection working with confidence scores
- [ ] Content theme classification from actual images (not just captions)
- [ ] Sentiment analysis output present
- [ ] Authenticity/fake detection working
- [ ] Mobile proxy IP in response metadata
- [ ] x402 payment flow
- [ ] Solana USDC wallet address

**No merge without a working deployed instance with real AI analysis output.**

## Market Context

| Competitor | Price | What You Get |
|-----------|-------|--------------|
| HypeAuditor | $199-499/month | Influencer analytics + fake detection |
| Modash | $199-999/month | Influencer search + analytics |
| Heepsy | $49-269/month | Influencer discovery |
| **This service** | **$0.15/analysis** | Full AI-powered profile + vision + authenticity |

At $0.15 per full analysis, a brand could analyze 1,000 potential influencers for $150 — vs $499/month for HypeAuditor's limited plan.

## Bounty: $200 in $SX token

This is the flagship bounty. It combines scraping + AI vision into a premium product. The difficulty and value justify the highest payout. First working deployed submission wins.

title: [BOUNTY] X/Twitter Real-Time Search API — $100 paid in $SX token
body:
# X/Twitter Real-Time Search API — $100 Bounty

## What to Build

An API that searches tweets by keyword/hashtag, gets trending topics by region, extracts user profiles and engagement metrics, and monitors conversations — all without X's $42,000/year official API.

X's API pricing created a massive gap: free tier is too limited, Basic ($100/month) is restrictive, Pro ($5,000/month) is enterprise-only. This service fills the gap at micropayment prices.

## Why Mobile Proxies Are Required

X.com rolls out anti-scraping changes every 2-4 weeks. Guest tokens expire, rate limits shift constantly. Mobile carrier IPs are critical because X's mobile app generates the majority of traffic, so mobile IPs face significantly less scrutiny. X's detection system profiles request patterns by IP type — mobile IPs get the most generous rate limits (5-10x more than datacenter/residential).

## Endpoints

```
GET /api/x/search?query=keyword&sort=latest&limit=20
GET /api/x/trending?country=US
GET /api/x/user/:handle
GET /api/x/user/:handle/tweets?limit=20
GET /api/x/thread/:tweet_id
```

## Expected Response (Search)

```json
{
  "query": "mobile proxies",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "1234567890",
      "author": {
        "handle": "example",
        "name": "Example User",
        "followers": 12500,
        "verified": false
      },
      "text": "Full tweet text...",
      "created_at": "2026-02-15T10:30:00Z",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 8,
      "views": 15000,
      "url": "https://x.com/example/status/1234567890",
      "media": [],
      "hashtags": ["mobileproxies"]
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "total_results": 20,
    "proxy": { "ip": "...", "country": "US", "carrier": "T-Mobile" }
  }
}
```

## Pricing

- `$0.01 USDC` per search query
- `$0.005 USDC` per trending topics fetch
- `$0.01 USDC` per user profile
- `$0.02 USDC` per thread extraction

## Technical Requirements

1. Route all X/Twitter requests through Proxies.sx mobile proxies
2. Search tweets by keyword, hashtag, or from:user
3. Get trending topics by country/region
4. Extract user profiles with follower counts and verification status
5. Thread extraction (full conversation thread from a tweet ID)
6. Handle X's constantly changing anti-scraping measures
7. Rate limiting resilience — demonstrate 20+ consecutive successful queries
8. Wire into `src/service.ts` with x402 payment flow

## Submission Requirements

- [ ] Live deployment URL
- [ ] Real tweet data from search queries (not mock data)
- [ ] Trending topics from at least 2 countries
- [ ] User profile extraction working
- [ ] 20+ consecutive successful queries demonstrated
- [ ] x402 payment flow
- [ ] Solana USDC wallet address

**No merge without a working deployed instance.**

## Market Context

- X Official API: $100/month (Basic, very limited) to $42,000/year (Pro)
- SociaVault: $47-200/month
- ScrapeCreators: $49/month
- Your service: $0.01/query via micropayment — accessible to AI agents and researchers
- Every brand, journalist, researcher, and AI agent needs real-time X data

## Bounty: $100 in $SX token

Hard bounty — X changes anti-scraping weekly. But the demand is enormous and the payout reflects the difficulty. First working deployed submission wins.

title: [BOUNTY] Google Discover Feed Intelligence API — $75 paid in $SX token
body:
## Bounty: Google Discover Feed Intelligence API

**Reward:** $75 paid in $SX token

---

### What to Build

An API that captures and returns Google Discover feed content as seen from real mobile devices in specific countries. Google Discover is **exclusively mobile** — it does not exist on desktop. There is no official API. The only way to access this data is through real mobile devices on real carrier networks.

This means **zero competition** — nobody else can offer this data at scale without real mobile infrastructure.

### Endpoint

```
GET /api/run?country=US&category=technology
GET /api/run?country=DE&category=news
GET /api/run?country=GB&category=sports
```

### Required Output Schema

```json
{
  "country": "US",
  "category": "technology",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-14T12:00:00Z",
  "discover_feed": [
    {
      "position": 1,
      "title": "Article title from Discover",
      "source": "Publisher Name",
      "sourceUrl": "https://publisher.com",
      "url": "https://publisher.com/article",
      "snippet": "Preview text shown in Discover",
      "imageUrl": "https://...",
      "contentType": "article",
      "publishedAt": "2026-02-14T08:00:00Z",
      "category": "Technology",
      "engagement": {
        "hasVideoPreview": false,
        "format": "standard"
      }
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "feedLength": 20,
    "scrapedAt": "2026-02-14T12:00:05Z",
    "proxyCountry": "US",
    "proxyCarrier": "T-Mobile"
  },
  "proxy": { "country": "US", "carrier": "T-Mobile", "type": "mobile" },
  "payment": { "txHash": "...", "amount": 0.02, "verified": true }
}
```

### Technical Requirements

| Requirement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| **Mobile proxy** | Must use Proxies.sx `proxyFetch()` — real 4G/5G carrier IPs only. Discover literally does not render on non-mobile connections |
| **Browser rendering** | Must use a real headless browser (Playwright/Puppeteer) with mobile viewport and user-agent. Discover is JavaScript-rendered |
| **x402 payment** | Full 402 → pay → `Payment-Signature` → data flow |
| **Country support** | At minimum US, DE, GB. All 6 countries (US, DE, FR, ES, GB, PL) preferred |
| **Content extraction** | Title, source, URL, snippet, image URL, content type, publish date |
| **Category filtering** | Support filtering by topic/category if Google surfaces it |
| **Pricing** | Suggested $0.01-0.03/query |

### Data Points to Extract

- [ ] Feed articles with title, source, URL, snippet
- [ ] Content type detection (article, video, web story)
- [ ] Publisher/source information
- [ ] Image/thumbnail URLs
- [ ] Category/topic classification
- [ ] Feed position/ranking
- [ ] Published date
- [ ] Country-specific trending topics — **bonus**
- [ ] Comparison across countries (same topic, different Discover rankings) — **bonus**

### Why Mobile Proxies Are Mandatory

This is the strongest mobile-proxy use case in the marketplace:

1. **Discover is MOBILE-ONLY** — there is literally no desktop version. No API. No web interface. It only appears in the Google app and Chrome mobile
2. **Carrier-level personalization** — Discover content differs based on carrier network (T-Mobile US shows different trending content than Vodafone DE)
3. **No alternatives exist** — SEO tools like Ahrefs, SEMrush, and Moz have **zero** Discover data because they can't access it without real mobile devices
4. **Google's Feb 2026 Discover Core Update** emphasizes locally relevant content, making geo-specific mobile data even more valuable

### Market Context

Publishers, content marketers, and SEO agencies have **no visibility** into what Google Discover surfaces. Discover drives massive traffic (often more than Search for news publishers) but there's no way to track it. This service would be the first of its kind.

SEO monitoring tools market: $1.5B+ annually. A Discover-specific tool fills a gap that every major SEO platform lacks.

### How to Claim

1. Fork this repo and build the service
2. **Deploy a live instance** — provide a public URL we can test against
3. **Show proof**: Post real Discover feed JSON from at least 3 countries (US, DE, GB) showing actual articles with real publishers, real URLs, real publish dates
4. **Demonstrate the content differs by country** — show that US feed ≠ DE feed ≠ GB feed
5. **Demonstrate resilience**: 10+ consecutive successful feed captures
6. Provide your Solana USDC wallet address
7. Submit a PR with your code

**No merge without a working deployed app.**

---

**Status:** OPEN
